page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5ne.4xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance configured on the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8163 processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The instance provides 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 7 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 25 percent, and single-threaded gzip compression registering in the bottom 10 percent. However, multi-core execution scales to average levels, with stress-ng multi-core, PassMark CPU, and Geekbench multi-core scores all placing in the middle 50 percent. Memory bandwidth tests show average read and write speeds, though the overall PassMark memory score is positioned in the bottom 25 percent.

Given the dedicated CPU allocation and balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio, this instance is suited for multi-threaded workloads that do not require high single-core burst speeds. Suitable applications include static web serving, Redis key-value databases, and moderate database operations, which all perform within average parameters. The absence of local storage means workloads must rely on network-attached storage, and the lack of a GPU limits its utility for heavy deep learning tasks, though it can handle basic text generation on smaller language models at average speeds.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5ne.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz on the KVM hypervisor. The hardware configuration provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between weak single-core execution and average multi-core processing capabilities. Memory bandwidth and database operations perform in the middle tier, while single-threaded tasks are relatively slow. With a baseline network bandwidth of 7 Gbps, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for multi-threaded workloads that require dedicated CPU resources without extreme single-core demands. It is best suited for static web serving, Redis deployments, and general-purpose database operations.

tagline: A dedicated x86 virtual server offering balanced compute and memory resources for multi-threaded workloads without local storage requirements.
